resolution_info.auto: Tries your source resolution first, then drops if needed to hit the size target or quality floor.
resolution_info.2160: 4K - maximum pixel quality. Very large files; needs a generous size limit.
resolution_info.1080: 1080p - great quality for most content. May need heavy compression for small targets.
resolution_info.720: 720p - strong balance. For fast gameplay, often looks sharper than compressed 1080p.
resolution_info.540: 540p - noticeably smaller. Good for short clips or tight size limits.
resolution_info.480: 480p - small files. Acceptable for most sharing.
resolution_info.360: 360p - very small files. Best for strict limits.

av1_nvenc.label: AV1  - NVIDIA GPU
av1_nvenc.desc: Best compression, smallest files. Requires RTX 20xx or newer.

av1_amf.label: AV1  - AMD GPU
av1_amf.desc: Best compression, smallest files. Requires RX 6000 series or newer.

libaom-av1.label: AV1  - CPU (slow)
libaom-av1.desc: Best compression but extremely slow - a 10-min video can take hours.

hevc_nvenc.label: H.265 - NVIDIA GPU
hevc_nvenc.desc: ~40% smaller than H.264 at equal quality. Fast. Note: some Discord users get a download link instead of inline playback.

hevc_amf.label: H.265 - AMD GPU
hevc_amf.desc: ~40% smaller than H.264 at equal quality. Fast. Note: some Discord users get a download link instead of inline playback.

libx265.label: H.265 - CPU
libx265.desc: ~40% smaller than H.264 at equal quality. Slower than GPU. Note: some Discord users get a download link instead of inline playback.

h264_nvenc.label: H.264 - NVIDIA GPU
h264_nvenc.desc: Maximum compatibility. Plays everywhere including Discord inline. Fast.

h264_amf.label: H.264 - AMD GPU
h264_amf.desc: Maximum compatibility. Plays everywhere including Discord inline. Fast.

libx264.label: H.264 - CPU
libx264.desc: Maximum compatibility. Plays everywhere including Discord inline. Slower than GPU.
